title: Pelican to Murder — Chapter 1: The First Lie
description: The investigation begins with what looks like a simple service-rail murder at Pelican to Mars. Detectives learn the basic case logic: object movement, story time, opportunity, and one suspect whose small lie exposes the first layer of a larger operation called Black Pelican.

answerKey.method: Served Lena the unmarked praline cocktail after moving the allergy marker
answerKey.motive: To keep Lena from replacing her as lead event bartender
answerKey.keyEvidence: Allergy marker text, service rail photo, and register camera still
answerKey.explanation: Tessa knew the blue-pick allergy system and was the only person confirmed at the service rail when Lena’s marked glass became unmarked. Her text made the marker important, the photo proved the marker moved, and the register camera placed her at the swap.

publicClues.title: Chapter 1 Opened

publicClues.title: Black Pelican Reference
publicClues.text: At 9:04 PM, the final register export includes a small sponsor adjustment tagged BLACK PELICAN. It does not prove Tessa killed Lena, but it proves Lena had been looking at something larger than one bad drink.

rounds.title: Round 1: The Drink Marker
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the drink marker and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 1 is open. This chapter stands on its own, but every clue may also reveal how the larger Black Pelican story is expanding.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 1 is nearly complete. Lock in what the drink marker proves before the next round opens.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Chapter 1 is open: The First Lie. Solve this chapter’s immediate case, but watch for the thread that connects to The Pelican Files.
rounds.liveActionTitle: The first argument breaks through the bar noise.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Tessa wipes down the service rail too quickly, then snaps, “I already said Lena’s glass was marked.” A barback points toward the garnish tray and quietly answers, “Then why is the blue pick over there instead of in the drink?”

rounds.title: Round 2: The Service Rail
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the service rail and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 2 is open. Focus on the service rail. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 2 is nearly complete. Lock in what the service rail proves before the next round opens.
rounds.breakingUpdate: A new contradiction has surfaced. Compare the latest clue against the suspect statements before answering the checkpoint.
rounds.liveActionTitle: The staff starts watching each other.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Owen steps out of the office as Tessa and another bartender stop talking. No one accuses anyone out loud, but the room changes: everyone is suddenly aware that the service rail matters.

rounds.title: Round 3: The Register Camera
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the register camera and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 3 is open. Focus on the register camera. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 3 is nearly complete. Lock in what the register camera proves before the next round opens.
rounds.breakingUpdate: The evidence path is widening. Look for the clue that connects a claim to a physical record.
rounds.liveActionTitle: A photo makes the silence worse.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: A detective places the register camera still on the table. Tessa leans in, sees the timestamp, and looks away before anyone asks the next question.

rounds.title: Round 4: The Job Threat
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the job threat and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 4 is open. Focus on the job threat. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 4 is nearly complete. Lock in what the job threat proves before the next round opens.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Pressure is building. A suspicious detail may be a red herring unless it connects method, motive, and opportunity.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: Someone near the bar mutters that Lena was planning staffing changes after tonight. Tessa hears it and says, “That was just talk,” but her voice loses its edge.

rounds.title: Round 5: The Unmarked Glass
rounds.objective: Review evidence connected to the unmarked glass and answer the checkpoint question.
rounds.dialogue: Round 5 is open. Focus on the unmarked glass. The clues in this round are built to test one part of the case logic.
rounds.countdownReview: Checkpoint 5 is nearly complete. Lock in this chapter’s case logic before the final accusation connects the bigger arc.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Final round of Chapter 1: the immediate case and the larger story thread are converging.
rounds.liveActionTitle: One glass becomes the center of the room.
rounds.liveActionDialogue: The unmarked glass is bagged as evidence. The bar goes quiet as the detective notes that nobody has explained how it reached Lena without the allergy marker attached.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Answer the final five questions to close the case.
rounds.dialogue: The final accusation window is open. Choose the culprit, method, motive, key evidence, and the statement that fails.
rounds.countdownReview: The final window is closing. Submit all answers before the case locks.
rounds.breakingUpdate: Final round evidence is live. One detail should help separate the loudest suspect from the provable answer.

openingNarration: Tonight begins with the death of Lena at Pelican to Mars. At first, the case looks contained: one drink, one missing allergy marker, one bartender with something to lose. But the first case file also introduces a strange sponsor note marked Black Pelican. Solve the immediate murder first. The larger story begins with the first lie.
revealScript:
Case closed. This was Level 1: Training Detective Case. The culprit was Tessa Marchand. The method: Served Lena the unmarked praline cocktail after moving the allergy marker. The motive: To keep Lena from replacing her as lead event bartender. The proof: Allergy marker text, service rail photo, and register camera still.

Continuing Story Thread: The case closes on Tessa, but the recovered register export includes a sponsor reference nobody can explain: BLACK PELICAN. That thread carries into Chapter 2.

seriesArc.nextThread: Chapter 2 asks why the Black Pelican sponsor file was being hidden.

lobbyBackstory: You have been called to Pelican to Mars after a private event turns into a crime scene. At first, it looks like one bad drink and one missing allergy marker, but the staff is already whispering about a hidden sponsor file called Black Pelican. When the investigation begins, watch the timeline, compare alibis, and use your Detective Notes before each checkpoint.
